query: Has the NFL placed Terrion Arnold on the Commissioner's Exempt List as of July 2026? Latest news on Detroit Lions cornerback Terrion Arnold NFL status after June 2026 arrest.
answer:
## Summary

**Has the NFL placed Terrion Arnold on the Commissioner's Exempt List as of July 2026? — No.**

As of the latest reporting (July 1, 2026):

- **No source confirms Arnold has been placed on the Exempt List.** The NFL's consistent official position is that "**the matter remains under review**."
- The question is effectively **moot** because the **Detroit Lions waived him** — officially processed on the NFL transaction wire July 1, 2026, with a **waiver claim deadline of Monday, July 6, 2026 (4:00 p.m. ET)**. The Commissioner's Exempt List is a **paid-leave tool for rostered players**, so it does not currently apply to a player who is off the roster.
- NBC Sports explicitly framed it conditionally: *"If Arnold were on a roster, he would likely face a paid leave by the NFL."* All Exempt List references in coverage are prospective ("could be placed"), never confirmations.

**Key context on the June 2026 arrest:**
- Charged with **8 felony counts** (4 kidnapping, 4 armed robbery) tied to an alleged Feb. 4, 2026 Tampa kidnapping/robbery; max sentence is life in prison. He denies all charges.
- Turned himself in June 24, charged June 25, granted a **$1 million bond** June 29 (house arrest, passport surrender, no ankle monitor).
- Lions moved to cut him hours after the bond ruling; waiving created dead cap hits of ~$6.6M (2026) and ~$4.5M (2027).
- All outlets consider a waiver claim highly unlikely given his legal exposure and remaining ~$14.3M contract. A pending court motion seeks to require an ankle monitor now that he is no longer NFL-employed.

Full findings, sources, and structured data points have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Lions officially waive Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: The Detroit Lions officially waived cornerback Terrion Arnold, processed on the NFL transaction report July 1, 2026, with a claiming deadline of Monday, July 6, at 4:00 p.m. ET. If Arnold were on a roster, he would likely face a paid leave by the NFL.

sources.title: NFL says Terrion Arnold case 'remains under review'
sources.snippet: A league spokesperson stated 'The matter remains under review.' The NFL's Personal Conduct Policy permits the Commissioner to place a player on paid leave if charged with a felony or crime of violence.

sources.title: Lions Officially Cut CB Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: The move sends Arnold to waivers... The NFL is reviewing the matter, and Arnold could potentially be placed on the commissioner's exempt list.

sources.title: Lions Make Terrion Arnold Decision Official
sources.snippet: Waived rather than released; other teams have until Monday, July 6, at 4 PM EST to submit waiver requests. The waiver results in dead cap hits of $6.6 million in 2026 and $4.5 million in 2027.

sources.title: NFL player charged with kidnapping gets 'tremendous benefit' in ruling to keep job
sources.snippet: Judge Sabella granted bond June 29, rejected GPS monitor. The NFL has yet to determine Arnold's status, though he is a potential candidate for the Commissioner's Exempt list.

sources.title: 'We Gotta Move On': Detroit Lions Fans React to Terrion Arnold Being Cut
sources.snippet: The NFL has not yet determined Arnold's playing future, with a league spokesman stating the matter is under advisement. Arnold could potentially be placed on the Commissioner's Exempt List.

sources.title: The Best (And Worst) Options Lions Can Take Regarding Punishing Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: The team must choose between keeping him on the roster, cutting him, or placing him on the Commissioner's Exempt List. Commissioner Roger Goodell alone has authority to place a player on the Exempt List.

sources.title: Lions CB Terrion Arnold arrested on felony kidnapping, robbery charges
sources.snippet: State Attorney Susan S. Lopez announced 8 felony charges; potential life sentence. The NFL has contacted the team to discuss the matter.

sources.title: Lions Release Terrion Arnold After First-Round Pick Faces Eight Felony Counts in Tampa
sources.snippet: 8 felony counts under Florida Statutes 787.01 (kidnapping) and 812.13 (armed robbery); $1M bail set June 29 by Judge Sabella; Nick Saban submitted character reference letter.

sources.title: 2x Super Bowl Champ Claims Lions Might Have Insider Info After Terrion Arnold Roster Decision
sources.snippet: Lions waived Arnold hours after bond ruling; prosecution filed motion to revoke his no-tracker privileges; courts to decide the upcoming week on ankle monitor.

key_facts: As of July 1, 2026, there is NO report that Terrion Arnold has been placed on the NFL Commissioner's Exempt List; all references are conditional/prospective.
The NFL's official and consistent statement (via league spokesperson to Pro Football Talk, June 29, 2026) is: 'The matter remains under review.'
The Detroit Lions waived Arnold; the transaction was officially processed on the NFL wire Wednesday, July 1, 2026, with a waiver claim deadline of Monday, July 6, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. ET.
Because Arnold is no longer on a roster, the Commissioner's Exempt List (a paid-leave tool for rostered players) does not currently apply to him.
NBC Sports (July 1, 2026) explicitly framed it conditionally: 'If Arnold were on a roster, he would likely face a paid leave by the NFL.'
The Exempt List can only be applied at Commissioner Roger Goodell's sole discretion under the Personal Conduct Policy.
Arnold faces 8 felony counts (4 kidnapping, 4 armed robbery) from an alleged Feb 4, 2026 Tampa kidnapping/robbery; maximum sentence is life in prison.
He turned himself in June 24, 2026; was charged June 25; and was granted a $1 million bond by Judge Christopher Sabella on June 29, 2026 (house arrest, passport surrender, no ankle monitor).
The Lions moved to cut Arnold hours after his bond was granted on June 29; waiving him created dead cap hits reported at ~$6.6M (2026) and ~$4.5M (2027).
All outlets consider it highly unlikely any team will claim Arnold off waivers given his legal exposure and remaining ~$14.3M rookie contract.
Arnold and his legal team (attorneys R. Timothy Jansen and Harvey Steinberg; agent Denise White of EAG Sports Management) categorically deny all charges.

Dead cap hit from waiving Arnold (2027)
